AL.disclosure_summary: Alabama does not have specific radon disclosure requirements for real estate transactions.
AL.license_note: Alabama does not require state licensing for radon professionals. Look for NRPP or AARST-certified professionals.

AK.disclosure_summary: Alaska does not mandate radon disclosure. However, general property disclosure requirements apply.
AK.license_note: Alaska does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

AZ.disclosure_summary: Arizona requires sellers to disclose all known material facts about the property, including radon test results, via the Seller's Property Disclosure Statement (SPDS).
AZ.license_note: Arizona does not require state licensing for radon professionals. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

AR.disclosure_summary: Arkansas does not have specific radon disclosure requirements in real estate transactions.
AR.license_note: Arkansas does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

CA.disclosure_summary: California requires sellers to complete the Transfer Disclosure Statement (TDS), which covers known environmental hazards including radon.
CA.license_note: California does not require specific radon licensing. Look for NRPP or AARST-certified professionals.

CO.disclosure_summary: Colorado requires sellers to provide the Seller's Property Disclosure form, which includes radon. Colorado has some of the highest radon levels in the nation due to its geology.
CO.license_note: Colorado does not require state licensing but strongly recommends using NRPP or AARST-certified professionals.

CT.disclosure_summary: Connecticut requires sellers to disclose known radon levels through the Residential Property Condition Disclosure Report.
CT.license_note: Connecticut requires radon testers and mitigators to be certified by the Connecticut DPH.

DE.disclosure_summary: Delaware requires sellers to disclose known material defects including radon through the Seller's Disclosure of Real Property Condition Report.
DE.license_note: Delaware does not require specific radon licensing. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

FL.disclosure_summary: Florida does not mandate radon disclosure in real estate transactions, but the Florida Building Code requires radon-resistant construction in Zone 1 counties.
FL.license_note: Florida does not require state licensing for radon professionals. Look for NRPP or AARST-certified professionals.

GA.disclosure_summary: Georgia does not have specific radon disclosure requirements. General property disclosure laws apply.
GA.license_note: Georgia does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

HI.disclosure_summary: Hawaii requires sellers to complete a Seller's Real Property Disclosure Statement covering known defects and environmental hazards.
HI.license_note: Hawaii does not require specific radon licensing.

ID.disclosure_summary: Idaho requires sellers to complete a Property Condition Disclosure Form, which covers known environmental hazards including radon.
ID.license_note: Idaho does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

IL.disclosure_summary: Illinois requires sellers to disclose known radon test results to buyers under the Residential Real Property Disclosure Act.
IL.license_note: Illinois requires radon mitigation professionals to be licensed by the Illinois Emergency Management Agency (IEMA).

IN.disclosure_summary: Indiana requires sellers to complete the Residential Real Estate Sales Disclosure Form, including any known radon test results.
IN.license_note: Indiana requires radon measurement and mitigation professionals to be licensed by the Indiana State Department of Health.

IA.disclosure_summary: Iowa requires radon testing results to be disclosed during residential property sales. Iowa has some of the highest average radon levels in the US.
IA.license_note: Iowa requires certification for radon measurement and mitigation professionals.

KS.disclosure_summary: Kansas requires sellers to disclose known material defects through the Seller's Disclosure Statement.
KS.license_note: Kansas requires radon professionals to be certified by the KDHE.

KY.disclosure_summary: Kentucky requires sellers to complete a Seller's Disclosure of Property Condition form, covering known environmental hazards.
KY.license_note: Kentucky does not require state licensing for radon professionals. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

LA.disclosure_summary: Louisiana does not have specific radon disclosure requirements in real estate transactions.
LA.license_note: Louisiana does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

ME.disclosure_summary: Maine requires sellers to disclose known defects through the Property Disclosure Statement, including radon test results.
ME.license_note: Maine does not require specific radon licensing. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

MD.disclosure_summary: Maryland requires sellers to disclose environmental hazards including radon through the Residential Property Disclosure and Disclaimer Statement.
MD.license_note: Maryland does not require specific radon licensing but recommends NRPP or AARST-certified professionals.

MA.disclosure_summary: Massachusetts does not have a mandatory seller disclosure form. Radon disclosure is not specifically required but concealment of known issues creates legal liability.
MA.license_note: Massachusetts does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

MI.disclosure_summary: Michigan requires sellers to complete a Seller's Disclosure Statement that includes environmental hazards such as radon.
MI.license_note: Michigan does not require specific radon licensing. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

MN.disclosure_summary: Minnesota requires radon disclosure at the time of sale. The Minnesota Department of Health provides extensive radon resources for homebuyers and sellers.
MN.license_note: Minnesota requires radon mitigation professionals to be licensed by the Minnesota Department of Health.

MS.disclosure_summary: Mississippi does not have specific radon disclosure requirements in real estate transactions.
MS.license_note: Mississippi does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

MO.disclosure_summary: Missouri requires sellers to complete a Seller's Disclosure Statement, which includes known environmental hazards.
MO.license_note: Missouri requires radon professionals to be licensed by the Missouri Department of Health and Senior Services.

MT.disclosure_summary: Montana does not have specific radon disclosure requirements. General property disclosure laws apply.
MT.license_note: Montana does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

NE.disclosure_summary: Nebraska requires sellers to disclose known defects through the Seller Property Condition Disclosure Statement.
NE.license_note: Nebraska requires radon professionals to be certified by the NDHHS.

NV.disclosure_summary: Nevada requires sellers to complete a Seller's Real Property Disclosure Form covering environmental hazards.
NV.license_note: Nevada does not require specific radon licensing.

NH.disclosure_summary: New Hampshire requires sellers to disclose known environmental hazards. The NH DHHS offers radon testing resources.
NH.license_note: New Hampshire does not require specific radon licensing. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

NJ.disclosure_summary: New Jersey requires radon testing and disclosure for all real estate transactions in Tier 1 counties under the Radon Hazard Subcode.
NJ.license_note: New Jersey requires certification for radon testers and mitigators through the NJ DEP.

NM.disclosure_summary: New Mexico requires sellers to complete a Property Condition Disclosure Statement including known environmental hazards.
NM.license_note: New Mexico does not require specific radon licensing.

NY.disclosure_summary: New York does not have a state-mandated radon disclosure requirement, but sellers must complete the Property Condition Disclosure Statement, and failing to disclose known issues can create legal liability.
NY.license_note: New York does not require state licensing for radon professionals, but the NRPP and AARST certifications are industry standard.

NC.disclosure_summary: North Carolina requires sellers to complete a Residential Property and Owners' Association Disclosure Statement, covering environmental hazards.
NC.license_note: North Carolina does not require specific radon licensing. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

ND.disclosure_summary: North Dakota requires sellers to complete a Seller's Property Condition Disclosure Statement.
ND.license_note: North Dakota does not require specific radon licensing.

OH.disclosure_summary: Ohio requires sellers to disclose known radon levels on the Ohio Residential Property Disclosure Form.
OH.license_note: Ohio requires radon testers and mitigators to be licensed by the Ohio Department of Health.

OK.disclosure_summary: Oklahoma requires sellers to complete a Residential Property Condition Disclosure Statement covering known defects.
OK.license_note: Oklahoma does not require specific radon licensing.

OR.disclosure_summary: Oregon requires sellers to complete a Seller's Property Disclosure Statement, which includes environmental hazards.
OR.license_note: Oregon does not require specific radon licensing. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

PA.disclosure_summary: Pennsylvania requires radon disclosure through the Seller's Property Disclosure Statement. The PA DEP also provides radon-specific guidance for real estate transactions.
PA.license_note: Pennsylvania requires certification for radon testing and mitigation professionals through the PA DEP.

RI.disclosure_summary: Rhode Island requires sellers to disclose known environmental hazards through the Residential Real Estate Sales Disclosure.
RI.license_note: Rhode Island does not require specific radon licensing.

SC.disclosure_summary: South Carolina requires sellers to complete a Seller's Disclosure of Property Condition, covering known defects.
SC.license_note: South Carolina does not require specific radon licensing.

SD.disclosure_summary: South Dakota requires sellers to disclose known property conditions through a Property Condition Disclosure Statement.
SD.license_note: South Dakota does not require specific radon licensing.

TN.disclosure_summary: Tennessee requires sellers to complete a Residential Property Condition Disclosure covering known defects and hazards.
TN.license_note: Tennessee does not require specific radon licensing.

TX.disclosure_summary: Texas does not have specific radon disclosure requirements. Most Texas counties are in EPA Zone 3 (low risk), but isolated areas can have elevated readings.
TX.license_note: Texas does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

UT.disclosure_summary: Utah requires sellers to disclose known defects through the Seller's Property Condition Disclosure.
UT.license_note: Utah does not require specific radon licensing.

VT.disclosure_summary: Vermont requires sellers to disclose known lead and environmental hazards. Radon falls under general disclosure obligations.
VT.license_note: Vermont does not require specific radon licensing.

VA.disclosure_summary: Virginia requires sellers to complete a Residential Property Disclosure Statement covering known defects and environmental hazards including radon.
VA.license_note: Virginia does not require specific radon licensing. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

WA.disclosure_summary: Washington requires sellers to complete a Seller Disclosure Statement (Form 17) covering environmental hazards.
WA.license_note: Washington does not require specific radon licensing.

WV.disclosure_summary: West Virginia does not have specific radon disclosure requirements. General caveat emptor laws apply.
WV.license_note: West Virginia does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

WI.disclosure_summary: Wisconsin requires sellers to complete a Real Estate Condition Report that includes known radon test results and environmental hazards.
WI.license_note: Wisconsin does not require specific radon licensing. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

WY.disclosure_summary: Wyoming does not have specific radon disclosure requirements. General property disclosure laws apply.
WY.license_note: Wyoming does not require state licensing for radon professionals.

DC.disclosure_summary: Washington D.C. requires sellers to provide a Seller's Disclosure Statement covering known environmental conditions.
DC.license_note: D.C. does not require specific radon licensing. NRPP or AARST certification is recommended.

default_state_rule.disclosure_summary: This state does not have a specific radon disclosure law. However, general real estate disclosure requirements may still apply. Consult a local real estate attorney for guidance.
default_state_rule.license_note: This state does not require specific licensing for radon professionals. Look for NRPP (National Radon Proficiency Program) or AARST-certified professionals for quality assurance.
